'''Keanan Brand''' (born 8 January 1999) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} or winger for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and for [[Warrington Wolves]] and [[Leigh Centurions]] (on loan) in the [[Super League]].

In 2023, Brand appeared as a contestant on the [[Love Island (2015 TV series, series 9)|ninth series]] of ''[[Love Island (2015 TV series)|Love Island]]''.<ref>{{cite web |title=Meet Keanan |url=https://www.itv.com/loveisland/articles/meet-keanan |website=[[ITV (TV network)|ITV]] |access-date=23 February 2023 |date=23 February 2023 |archive-date=23 February 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230223223519/https://www.itv.com/loveisland/articles/meet-keanan |url-status=live }}</ref>

==Background==
Brand was born in [[Skelmersdale]], [[Lancashire]], England.

==Playing career==
===Widnes Vikings===
In 2018 he made his [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] début in the [[Super League]] against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref name="Super League: Hull Kingston Rovers v Widnes Vikings">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/ERLP4055387|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180411005225/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/ERLP4055387|url-status=dead|archive-date=April 11, 2018|title=Super League: Hull Kingston Rovers v Widnes Vikings|publisher=BBC|date=7 April 2018|accessdate=7 April 2018}}</ref>

He featured 32 times in 2019 in all competitions for Widnes and was part of the team that made it to Wembley in the AB Sundecks 1895 cup final. He was also nominated for the Championship Young Player of the Year award.

===Warrington Wolves===
On 30 September 2019 it was announced that Brand had signed for the [[Warrington Wolves]] along with [[Anthony Gelling]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49876807|title=Warrington Wolves: Super League club sign Anthony Gelling & Keanan Brand from Widnes|website=BBC Sport|accessdate=17 Dec 2020}}</ref>

=== Leigh Leopards ===
On 18 December 2020 newly promoted [[Leigh Leopards]] (then Leigh Centurions) signed Brand on  a season long loan from [[Warrington Wolves]] for the 2021 [[Super League|Betfred Super League]] season.<ref>{{cite web|title=Keanan Brand: Leigh Leopardssign Warrington Wolves centre on loan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55371500|website=BBC Sport|accessdate=19 Dec 2020}}</ref>

On 20 November 2021 it was announced that Brand had left Warrington and signed for Leigh in the [[RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]].<ref>{{Cite web|last=Gordon|first=James|date=2021-11-20|title=Leigh complete permanent signing of Keanan Brand from Warrington|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/keanan-brand-leigh-signing-warrington/|access-date=2021-11-20|website=LoveRugbyLeague|language=en-GB}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls (loan)===
On 29 February 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in the RFL Championship on a two-week short-term loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=29 Feb 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Leigh Leopards outside back Keanan Brand makes short-term loan move to Championship side Bradford Bulls|url=https://www.totalrl.com/leigh-leopards-outside-back-keanan-brand-makes-short-term-loan-move-to-championship-side-bradford-bulls/}}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings (loan)===
On 27 June 2024 it was reported that he had signed for Widnes in the RFL Championship on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=27 Jun 2024|title=Leigh Leopards duo make Widnes Vikings moves|url=https://www.totalrl.com/leigh-leopards-duo-make-widnes-vikings-moves/}}</ref>

==International==
He made his debut for {{rlnt|Ireland}} v {{rlnt|Scotland}} in October 2024.
